title: [InstCombine] Convert mem intrinsic with null into a noop
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by identifying that the core insight—null pointer arguments with undefined behavior make memory intrinsics no-ops—applies broadly across multiple intrinsic types and conditions. They extracted a reusable lambda `IsPointerUndefined` that checks for a constant null pointer in an address space where null is undefined, decoupling the logic from any specific intrinsic.

This allowed the optimization to cover:
- All memory intrinsics (`memcpy`, `memmove`, `memset`, atomic variants) via `AnyMemTransferInst`/`AnyMemSetInst`.
- Both source and destination pointer arguments.
- Various edge cases: volatile, large constant sizes, `mempcpy`, and attribute-guarded null pointer validity.

The patch inserts a length-is-zero assumption to preserve potential guard semantics, then removes the intrinsic. The approach demonstrates how to lift a pattern from a single case by isolating the invariant condition and applying it through shared base classes and helper functions, ensuring robustness across the intrinsic family.
